summary:
## Lane 2: IPC Handler Wrapping with withIpcResult

### Summary
Wrapped all 52 `ipcMain.handle()` callbacks across 7 domain IPC modules with `withIpcResult()` from `ipc-contract.ts`, so they return `IpcResult<T>` envelopes instead of throwing raw errors.

### Files Modified
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-store.ts` — 6 handlers wrapped
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-fs.ts` — 5 handlers wrapped (2 ipcMain.on skipped)
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-session.ts` — 2 handlers wrapped
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-window.ts` — 10 handlers wrapped
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-locale.ts` — 2 handlers wrapped
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-config.ts` — 7 handlers wrapped
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-init.ts` — 20 handlers wrapped (1 ipcMain.on skipped)

### Files Skipped (per mission)
- `ipc-helpers.ts` — shared helper, no IPC handlers
- `ipc-validation.ts` — validation-only, no IPC handlers

### What Changed
In each file:
1. Added `import { withIpcResult } from "./ipc-contract"`
2. Every `ipcMain.handle(CHANNEL, callback)` body is now wrapped: `return withIpcResult("operation.name", async () => { ... original body ... })`

### Verification
- ✅ `bun typecheck` via `tsgo -b`: **0 new type errors**
- All pre-existing errors in `ipc-contract.ts` (PluginTransportHandler/PluginTransportUnsub) remain unchanged
- No changes to public API signatures, ipcMain.on handlers, or existing business logic
